claim_text: Many observed failures stem from inter-agent interaction design problems, not just weaknesses in individual agents or verification gaps alone.
mechanism_text: The evidence supports a structural mechanism in which coordination among agents creates its own error pathways through interaction design, so failure cannot be reduced either to weak individual agents or to verification gaps alone.
bounds: This remains a broad structural mechanism rather than a fully isolated causal account; the evidence does not assign causal weight across pathways or prove one universal root cause.
